data.title: Iranian missiles hit five US refueling planes in Saudi Arabia.
data.description: Five U.S. Air Force refueling tankers were damaged in Iran's missile attack on the Prince Sultan airbase in Saudi Arabia, according to U.S. media reports citing U.S. officials. The reports stated that the tankers were damaged in recent Iranian missile attacks. A source said the aircraft were damaged but not completely destroyed and are currently undergoing repairs. No casualties were reported. The report did not specify the exact model of the tankers. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $180 million yesterday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $180 million yesterday (March 13th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $144 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $63.066 billion. The Fidelity ETF (FBTC) followed with a net inflow of $23.2414 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $10.99 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $91.826 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.43%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $56.136 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $26.6926 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $26.6926 million yesterday (March 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $32.3907 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.981 billion. This was followed by BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF ETHB, with a net inflow of $2.1633 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $45.6451 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF FETH, with a net outflow of $7.8614 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.377 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $12.258 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.81%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.789 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin Network Resilience: Study Highlights Cable Failure Impact
data.description: A recent study conducted by Cambridge University has revealed that Bitcoin's network would require a failure of 72%–92% of inter-country submarine cables to experience significant node disconnection. According to NS3.AI, the research indicated that over 87% of 68 verified cable faults resulted in less than 5% impact on nodes, and random failures showed almost no correlation with Bitcoin's price fluctuations. The study further noted that targeted attacks on crucial cables or the top five hosting providers could lead to similar disruptions with considerably less infrastructure being compromised.

data.title: BP Advances Kaskida Project Following Approval from U.S. Bureau of Ocean Energy Management
data.description: BP has announced its intention to proceed with the Kaskida project after receiving approval from the U.S. Bureau of Ocean Energy Management. According to Jin10, the project underwent a rigorous one-year review process before gaining the necessary clearance. This development marks a significant step forward for BP in its efforts to expand its operations in the region.

data.title: AI-Generated Content Funds Creator Payouts
data.description: X Product Lead Nikita Bier announced that an additional $335,000 was allocated to creators in this week's payout. According to NS3.AI, the funds originated from undisclosed sources, including AI-generated war videos and misinformation publishers. This allocation highlights the ongoing challenges in managing content creation and distribution in the digital age, where AI-generated materials can significantly impact financial decisions and content strategies.

data.title: Kalshi Partners with Cash App for Seamless Deposits
data.description: Kalshi has announced a partnership with Cash App, enabling users to deposit funds into their Kalshi accounts using the Cash App platform. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration aims to streamline the process for users, enhancing the ease and efficiency of managing their accounts. The integration is expected to provide a more convenient experience for Kalshi users, allowing them to leverage Cash App's widespread popularity and user-friendly interface.

data.title: POAP to Halt Badge Issuance Amid Platform Growth Constraints
data.description: POAP has announced that it will enter maintenance mode on March 16, ceasing the issuance of new badges. According to NS3.AI, this decision will not affect existing badge collections. Co-founder Isabel stated on X that the move is due to limitations on the platform's growth.

data.description: On Mar 14, 2026, 03:33 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,100 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,101.820068 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.28% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Immutable X to Unlock 9.62 Million Tokens on March 21
data.description: Immutable X is set to unlock approximately 9.62 million tokens on March 21 at 8:00 AM UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this information comes from the Web3 asset data platform RootData's token unlocking data. The unlocked tokens are valued at around $1.58 million.

data.description: On Mar 14, 2026, 03:15 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 71,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 71,006.820313 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.23% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Brother Maji increased his long position in Ethereum to 8,500 ETH, achieving a 75% win rate in a single month.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Huang Licheng, nicknamed "Maji Dage," added to his 25x leveraged long position in Ethereum this morning, bringing his position to 8,500 ETH. He currently has a floating profit of approximately $70,000, a liquidation price of approximately $2,038, and has placed 31 limit sell orders in the $2,117-$2,300 range. Furthermore, Maji Dage's monthly win rate is currently 75%, with 3 profitable and 1 losing trades out of 4 closed positions.

data.title: USDC issuance surpasses $80 billion for the first time, setting a new record.
data.description: According to Artemis data, the total issuance of USDC across the entire blockchain has exceeded $80 billion for the first time, currently standing at $81.083 billion, setting a new historical record.

data.title: The Trump administration has initiated the process of releasing U.S. oil reserves.
data.description: The Trump administration has initiated a process to massively utilize the U.S. emergency petroleum reserves, issuing a request to exchange 86 million barrels of crude oil. The Department of Energy stated in a statement Friday that oil drawn from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, as part of the 172 million barrel release plan announced Wednesday, is expected to begin flowing into the market by the end of next week. This release of oil reserves is expected to take four months to complete and is part of a 400 million barrel action plan coordinated with other countries. Under the exchange terms, companies will return the loaned oil to the Department of Energy in the future, paying an additional amount as a premium. The Department of Energy stated earlier this week that the Trump administration has planned to replenish the Strategic Petroleum Reserve with approximately 200 million barrels of oil over the next year, 20% more than the reserves currently being used. (Jinshi)

data.title: GE Vernova and Hitachi to Explore Nuclear Reactor Opportunities in Southeast Asia
data.description: GE Vernova and Hitachi have reached an agreement to investigate the potential deployment of small modular nuclear reactors in Southeast Asia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the collaboration between the two companies as they aim to address the region's growing energy needs with innovative nuclear technology. This partnership seeks to leverage the expertise of both firms in advancing sustainable energy solutions. The exploration of small modular reactors is part of a broader strategy to enhance energy security and reduce carbon emissions in Southeast Asia.

data.title: X Platform Product Manager: An additional $335,000 in creator incentives will be distributed, funded by incentives corresponding to those who fail to disclose information about AI-generated war videos and those who publish false information.
data.description: Nikita Bier, product manager of X platform, stated in an article on X platform that an additional $335,000 has been allocated to X platform creators this week as part of their creator revenue sharing. This funding primarily comes from: 1. users temporarily banned from receiving incentives for posting undisclosed AI-generated war videos; and 2. deductions from posts that received community warnings for publishing false information.

data.title: BP's Cascadia Project to Begin Oil Production in 2029
data.description: BP has announced plans to commence oil production from its ultra-deepwater development project, Cascadia, in 2029. According to Jin10, this initiative marks a significant step in BP's strategy to expand its deepwater operations. The Cascadia project is expected to enhance BP's production capabilities and contribute to meeting global energy demands. The project underscores BP's commitment to leveraging advanced technology for efficient resource extraction in challenging environments. Further details on the project's progress and potential impact on the energy market are anticipated as the start date approaches.

data.title: South Africa Prepares for Increased Shipping Traffic Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: South Africa's Maritime Safety Authority announced on March 13 that it is closely monitoring the impact of Middle East tensions on global shipping routes. According to Jin10, the authority expects an increase in shipping traffic along the South African coast and has made necessary preparations to support passing vessels. Since the United States and Israel launched a large-scale military operation against Iran on February 28, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has been disrupted. As a result, many cargo and oil tankers are opting to reroute via South Africa's Cape of Good Hope.

data.title: The POAP platform will enter maintenance mode on March 16th, during which time badge issuance will be unavailable. Existing badge collections will not be affected.
data.description: Isabel, co-founder of the on-chain badge protocol POAP, announced on the X platform that due to limited platform growth, the team has decided to transition POAP to maintenance mode starting March 16, 2026. This means: • For new issuers: After March 16, new issuers will no longer have access to the issuer interface. • For existing issuers: Existing tools can be used, and existing POAPs will remain intact. • For collectors: The collector interface will not be affected. • Technical support: APIs and existing integrations will continue to operate. • Core changes: The platform will no longer undergo active development. As resources allocated to the service are reduced, some operations may run slower. Furthermore, the team plans to develop a more underlying, open, and truly permissionless new standard for digital collectibles, and POAP may be integrated into the new system in the future (pending).

data.title: Kalshi has partnered with Cash App to facilitate user top-ups.
data.description: Kalshi, a prediction market platform, announced that it has partnered with Cash App to allow users to top up their accounts using Cash App Pay.
